title: Scope
description: General requirement that all buildings and structures resist wind loads per Chapters 26-31

data.rule: Buildings and other structures, including the main wind force resisting system (MWFRS) and all components and cladding (C&C) thereof, shall be designed and constructed to resist the wind loads determined in accordance with Chapters 26 through 31.
data.then: Design and construct to resist wind loads per Chapters 26 through 31

title: Tornado Load Requirements for Risk Category III and IV
description: Additional tornado load requirements for higher risk category buildings

data.rule: Risk Category III and IV buildings and other structures, including the MWFRS and all C&C thereof, shall also be designed and constructed to resist tornado loads determined in accordance with Chapter 32, as applicable.
data.then: Design and construct to resist tornado loads per Chapter 32

title: Basic Wind Parameters for Other Provisions
description: The provisions of this section define basic wind parameters for use with other provisions contained in this standard

data.rule: The provisions of this section define basic wind parameters for use with other provisions contained in this standard.
data.then: Use basic wind parameters defined herein with other provisions in this standard

title: User Note: Storm Shelter Designation
description: User note about storm shelter designation limitations under Chapter 26

data.rule: A building or other structure designed for wind loads determined exclusively in accordance with Chapter 26 cannot be designated as a storm shelter without meeting additional critical requirements provided in the applicable building code and ICC 500, the ICC/NSSA Standard for the Design and Construction of Storm Shelters. See Commentary Section C26.11 for an in-depth discussion on Storm Shelters.
data.then: Meet additional requirements per applicable building code and ICC 500 for storm shelter designation

title: Permitted Procedures
description: Design wind loads for buildings and other structures shall be determined using one of the procedures specified in this section

data.rule: The design wind loads for buildings and other structures, including the MWFRS and C&C elements thereof, shall be determined using one of the procedures as specified in this section. An outline of the overall process for the determination of the wind loads, including section references, is provided in Figure 26.1-1.
data.then: Determine design wind loads using one of the procedures specified in Sections 26.1.2.1 and 26.1.2.2

title: Main Wind Force Resisting System Procedures
description: Permitted procedures for determining MWFRS wind loads

data.rule: Wind loads for the MWFRS shall be determined using one of the following procedures:
data.then: Use one of: 1) Directional Procedure for buildings of all heights as specified in Chapter 27 for buildings meeting the requirements specified therein; 2) Envelope Procedure for low-rise buildings as specified in Chapter 28 for buildings meeting the requirements specified therein; 3) Directional Procedure for building appurtenances (rooftop structures and rooftop equipment) and other structures (such as solid freestanding walls and solid freestanding signs, chimneys, tanks, open signs, single-plane open frames, and trussed towers) as specified in Chapter 29; or 4) Wind Tunnel Procedure for all buildings and all other structures as specified in Chapter 31.

title: Components and Cladding Procedures
description: Permitted procedures for determining C&C wind loads

data.rule: Wind loads on C&C on all buildings and other structures shall be designed using one of the following procedures:
data.then: Use one of: 1) Analytical Procedures provided in Parts 1 through 5, as appropriate, of Chapter 30; or 2) Wind Tunnel Procedure as specified in Chapter 31.

title: Performance-Based Procedures
description: Requirements for using performance-based wind design procedures

data.rule: Wind design of buildings and other structures using performance-based procedures shall be permitted subject to the approval of the Authority Having Jurisdiction. The performance-based wind design procedures used shall, at a minimum, conform to Section 1.3.1.3.
data.then: Performance-based procedures permitted with AHJ approval, conforming to Section 1.3.1.3 at a minimum

title: Definitions Applicability
description: Scope of definitions in Section 26.2

data.rule: The following definitions apply to the provisions of Chapters 26 through 31.
data.then: Apply the following definitions to Chapters 26 through 31

data.definition: Acceptable to the Authority Having Jurisdiction.
The ASCE database (version 2022-1.0) of geocoded wind speed design data.

title: ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase User Note
description: Reference to external ASCE wind speed database

data.target: ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase of geocoded wind speed design data

data.definition: A horizontal (maximum slope of 2%) patio cover attached to the building wall at any height; it is different from an overhang, which is an extension of the roof surface.
Three-second gust speed at 33 ft (10 m) above the ground in Exposure C (see Section 26.7.3) as determined in accordance with Section 26.5.1.
A building supported on structural elements where wind can pass beneath the building.

A building that has the total area of openings in each wall that receives positive external pressure less than or equal to 4 ft² (0.37 m²) or 1% of the area of that wall, whichever is smaller. This condition is expressed for each wall by the following equation: A_o < 0.01A_g or 4 ft² (0.37 m²), whichever is smaller.

title: Enclosed Building Opening Requirement
description: Formula for determining if a building meets the enclosed building criteria

data.expression: A_o < 0.01*A_g or 4 ft^2 (0.37 m^2), whichever is smaller

A_o.source: total area of openings in the wall that receives positive external pressure

A_g.source: gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified

data.definition: An enclosed, partially enclosed, or partially open building that complies with the following conditions: 1. Mean roof height h less than or equal to 60 ft (18 m), and 2. Mean roof height h does not exceed the least horizontal dimension.

title: Figure 26.1-1. Outline of process for determining wind loads.
description: Flowchart showing the basic parameters for determining wind loads on both the MWFRS and C&C, including basic wind speed, wind directionality factor, exposure category, topographic factor, ground elevation above sea level, velocity pressures, gust effect factor, enclosure classification, and internal pressure coefficient. Shows procedures via Chapter 27 (Directional for all heights), Chapter 28 (Envelope for low-rise), Chapter 29 (Directional for appurtenances and other structures), Chapter 30 (C&C Envelope/Directional procedures), and Chapter 31 (Wind Tunnel for any building or other structure).

data.description: Flowchart showing the outline of process for determining wind loads. Chapter 26 General Requirements box lists basic parameters: basic wind speed V (Section 26.5, Figure 26.5-1), wind directionality factor K_d (Section 26.6), exposure category (Section 26.7), topographic factor K_zt (Section 26.8), ground elevation above sea level (Section 26.9), velocity pressures (Section 26.10), gust effect factor (Section 26.11), enclosure classification (Section 26.12), and internal pressure coefficient GC_pi (Section 26.13). MWFRS loads branch to Chapters 27, 28, 29, 31. C&C loads branch to Chapters 30, 31.
data.skip_reason: Complex flowchart diagram that cannot be represented as tabular or formula data

data.definition: A building having each wall at least 80% open. This condition is expressed for each wall by the equation A_o >= 0.8A_g, where A_o is the total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure, ft² (m²), and A_g is the gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified, ft² (m²).

title: Open Building Wall Opening Requirement
description: Formula for determining if a wall meets the open building criteria

A_o.source: total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure

A_g.source: gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified

data.definition: A building that complies with both of the following conditions: 1. Total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure exceeds the sum of the areas of openings in the balance of the building envelope (walls and roof) by more than 10%. 2. Total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure exceeds 4 ft² (0.37 m²) or 1% of the area of that wall, whichever is smaller, and the percentage of openings in the balance of the building envelope does not exceed 20%.

title: Partially Enclosed Building Conditions
description: Formulas expressing the conditions for a partially enclosed building

data.expression: A_o > 1.10*A_oi AND (A_o > 4 ft^2 or A_o > 0.01*A_g, whichever is smaller) AND A_oi/A_gi <= 0.20

A_o.source: total area of openings in a wall that receives positive external pressure

A_oi.source: sum of the areas of openings in the building envelope (walls and roof) not including A_o

A_g.source: gross surface area of that wall in which A_o is identified

A_gi.source: sum of the gross surface areas of the building envelope (walls and roof) not including A_g

data.definition: A building that does not comply with the requirements for open, partially enclosed, or enclosed buildings.

title: Building, Torsionally Regular Under Wind Load Definition

data.term: BUILDING, TORSIONALLY REGULAR UNDER WIND LOAD
data.definition: A building with the MWFRS about each principal axis proportioned so that the maximum displacement at each story under Case 2, the torsional wind load case, of Figure 27.3-8 does not exceed the maximum displacement at the same location under Case 1 of Figure 27.3-8, the basic wind load case.
Cladding, roofing, exterior walls, glazing, door assemblies, window assemblies, skylight assemblies, and other components enclosing the building.

title: Building or Other Structure, Flexible Definition

data.definition: Slender buildings and other structures that have a fundamental natural frequency less than 1 Hz.
Glazing that has been shown by testing to withstand the impact of test missiles. See Section 26.12.3.
With respect to topographic effects in Section 26.8, a land surface characterized by strong relief in any horizontal direction (see Figure 26.8-1).

Areas vulnerable to hurricanes in the United States and its territories. These are defined as 1. The US Atlantic Ocean and Gulf of Mexico coasts where the basic wind speed for Risk Category II buildings is greater than 115 mi/h (51.4 m/s), and 2. Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Guam, US Virgin Islands, Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.
Construction that has been shown by testing to withstand the impact of test missiles that is applied, attached, or locked over exterior glazing. See Section 26.12.3.
An assemblage of structural elements assigned to provide support and stability for the overall building or other structure. The system generally receives wind loading from more than one surface.

The average of the roof eave height and the height to the highest point on the roof surface, except that, for roof angles less than or equal to 10 degrees, the mean roof height is permitted to be taken as the roof eave height.
Holes that allow air to flow through the building envelope during a design wind event.
Published research findings and technical papers that are approved.
With respect to topographic effects in Section 26.8, an elongated crest of a hill characterized by strong relief in two directions.
A device to receive solar radiation and convert it into electricity or heat energy. Typically this is a photovoltaic module or solar thermal panel.
Any number of rooftop solar panels (solar close-spaced).
Areas within hurricane-prone regions where impact-resistant openings are required for glazed openings. See Section 26.12.3.

A procedure for determining wind loads on buildings and other structures in which pressures and/or forces and moments are determined for each wind direction considered, from a model of the building or other structure and its surroundings, in accordance with Chapter 31.

data.rule: The following symbols apply only to the provisions of Chapters 26 through 31.
data.then: The symbols and notation defined in Section 26.3 apply to Chapters 26 through 31.

data.rule: Positive pressure acts toward the surface, and negative pressure acts away from the surface.
Values of external and internal pressures shall be combined algebraically to determine the most critical load.

title: 26.4.3 Wind Pressures Acting on Opposite Faces of Each Building Surface

data.rule: In the calculation of design wind loads for the MWFRS and for C&C for buildings, the algebraic sum of the pressures acting on opposite faces of each building surface shall be taken into account.

The basic wind speed, V, used in the determination of design wind loads on buildings and other structures shall be determined from Figure 26.5-1 as follows, except as provided in Sections 26.5.1 and 26.5.2.

The basic wind speed V, used in the determination of design wind loads on buildings and other structures shall be determined from Figure 26.5-1 as follows, except as provided in Sections 26.5.1 and 26.5.2.
data.then: Use Figures 26.5-1A, see Figure 26.5-1A.

title: 26.5.1 Basic Wind Speed - Risk Category II

data.rule: For Risk Category II buildings and structures, use Figure 26.5-1B.
data.then: Use Figure 26.5-1B.

title: 26.5.1 Basic Wind Speed - Risk Category III

data.rule: For Risk Category III buildings and structures, use Figure 26.5-1C.
data.then: Use Figure 26.5-1C.

title: 26.5.1 Basic Wind Speed - Risk Category IV

data.rule: For Risk Category IV buildings and structures, use Figure 26.5-1D.
data.then: Use Figure 26.5-1D.

title: 26.5.1 Basic Wind Speed - Alternative (ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase)

data.rule: Alternatively, it shall be permitted to use the basic wind speeds from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase for the contiguous United States, Alaska, and selected special wind regions.

title: Figure 26.5-1A: Basic Wind Speeds for Risk Category I Buildings and Other Structures
description: Contour map of the United States showing basic wind speeds (3-s gust wind speeds in mph and m/s at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C) for Risk Category I buildings and other structures. Western US half on page 266, eastern US half (continued) on page 267. Includes Special Wind Region table and notes.

data.description: Basic wind speeds for Risk Category I. Wind speeds correspond to approximately a 15% probability of exceedance in 50 years (Annual Exceedance Probability = 0.0333, MRI = 300 years).
data.skip_reason: Contour map — geographic wind speed data requires GIS, not tabular extraction

metadata.qc_notes: Notes: 1. Values are 3-s gust wind speeds in mph (m/s) at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C. 2. Linear interpolation is permitted between contours. Point values are provided to aid with interpolation. 3. Islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour shall use the last wind speed contour. 4. Location-specific basic wind speeds shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase. 5. Wind speeds for Hawaii, US Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico shall be determined from the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase. 6. Mountainous terrain, gorges, ocean promontories, and special wind regions shall be examined for unusual wind conditions. Site-specific values for selected special wind regions shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase. 7. The ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase can be accessed at the ASCE 7 Hazard Tool (https://asce7hazardtool.online/) or approved equivalent. Special Wind Region data: American Samoa V=150 mph (67 m/s); Guam & Northern Mariana Islands V=180 mph (80 m/s); Hawaii — ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase; Puerto Rico — ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase; U.S. Virgin Islands — ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.

Western US half on page 268, eastern US half (continued) on page 269. Includes Special Wind Region table and notes.

title: Special Wind Regions
description: Requirements for special wind regions including mountainous terrain, gorges, and ocean promontories.

data.rule: Mountainous terrain, gorges, and special wind regions shown in Figure 26.5-1 shall be examined for unusual wind conditions. The Authority Having Jurisdiction shall, if necessary, adjust the values given in Figure 26.5-1 to account for higher local wind speeds. Such adjustment shall be based on meteorological information and an estimate of the basic wind speed obtained in accordance with the provisions of Section 26.5.3.
exceptions: Site-specific values for select special wind regions in the contiguous United States have been included in the ASCE 7 Wind Design Geodatabase. It shall be permitted to use the wind speed values for Special Wind Regions from the ASCE 7 Wind Design Geodatabase (https://asce7hazardtool.online/).

title: Estimation of Basic Wind Speeds from Regional Climatic Data
description: Conditions under which regional climatic data may be used to estimate basic wind speeds.

data.rule: Regional climatic data shall only be used in lieu of the basic wind speeds given in Figure 26.5-1 when (1) approved extreme-value statistical analysis procedures have been used in reducing the data, and (2) the length of record, sampling error, averaging time, anemometer height, data quality, and terrain exposure of the anemometer have been taken into account. Reduction in basic wind speed below that of Figure 26.5-1 shall be permitted.
exceptions: In hurricane-prone regions, wind speeds derived from simulations shall only be used in lieu of the basic wind speeds given in Figure 26.5-1 when appropriate simulation and extreme-value statistical analysis procedures are used. The use of regional wind-speed data obtained from anemometers is not permitted to define the hurricane wind-speed risk along the Gulf and Atlantic coasts, the Caribbean, or Hawaii.

When the basic wind speed is estimated from regional climatic data or simulation, the basic wind speed shall cover the applicable mean recurrence interval, and the estimate shall be adjusted for exposure to be a 3-s gust wind speed at 33 ft (10 m) above ground in Exposure C.

title: Wind Directionality Factor
description: Determination of the wind directionality factor Kd.

data.rule: The wind directionality factor, Kd, shall be determined from Table 26.6-1 and shall be included in the wind loads calculated in Chapter 27 through 30. The effect of wind directionality in determining wind loads in accordance with Chapter 31 shall be based on a rational analysis of the wind speeds conforming to the requirements of Section 26.5.3 and of Section 31.4.3.

title: Table 26.6-1. Wind Directionality Factor, Kd
description: Directionality factor Kd values for various structure types. Footnote: Directionality factor Kd = 0.90 shall be permitted for round or octagonal structures with round or octagonal structural systems.

rows.Structure Type: Buildings: Main wind force resisting system
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures: Square
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures: Hexagonal
Chimneys, tanks, and similar structures: Round
Solid freestanding walls, roof top equipment, and solid freestanding and attached signs
Open signs and single-plane open frames

title: Exposure
description: Requirements for determining upwind exposure for each wind direction.

data.rule: For each wind direction considered, the upwind exposure shall be based on ground surface roughness that is determined from natural topography, vegetation, and constructed facilities.

title: Wind Direction and Sectors
description: Defines how exposure is determined for each selected wind direction.

data.rule: For each selected wind direction at which the wind loads are to be determined, the exposure of the building or structure shall be determined for the two upwind sectors extending 45 degrees on either side of the selected wind direction. The exposure in these two sectors shall be determined in accordance with Sections 26.7.2 and 26.7.3, and the exposure the use of which would result in the highest wind loads shall be used to represent the winds from that direction.

title: Surface Roughness Categories
description: Definitions of surface roughness categories A, B, C, and D.

data.rule: A ground surface roughness within each 45-degree sector shall be determined for a distance upwind of the site, as defined in Section 26.7.3, from the categories defined in the following text, for the purpose of assigning an exposure category as defined in Section 26.7.3.

data.definition: Urban and suburban areas, wooded areas, or other terrain with numerous, closely spaced obstructions having the size of single-family dwellings or larger.
Open terrain with scattered obstructions having heights generally less than 30 ft (9.1 m). This category includes flat, open country and grasslands.
Flat, unobstructed areas and water surfaces. This category includes smooth mud flats, salt flats, and unbroken ice.

For buildings or other structures with a mean roof height less than or equal to 30 ft (9.1 m), Exposure B shall apply where the ground surface roughness, as defined by Surface Roughness B, prevails in the upwind direction for a distance greater than 1,500 ft (457 m). For buildings or other structures with a mean roof height greater than 30 ft (9.1 m), Exposure B shall apply where Surface Roughness B prevails in the upwind direction for a distance greater than 2,600 ft (792 m) or 20 times the height of the building or structure, whichever is greater.
Exposure C shall apply for all cases where Exposure B or D does not apply.

Exposure D shall apply where the ground surface roughness, as defined by Surface Roughness D, prevails in the upwind direction for a distance greater than 5,000 ft (1,524 m) or 20 times the building or structure height, whichever is greater. Exposure D shall also apply where the ground surface roughness immediately upwind of the site is B or C, and the building or structure is within a distance of 600 ft (183 m) or 20 times the building or structure height, whichever is greater, from an Exposure D condition as defined in the previous sentence.

title: Exposure Categories - Transition Zone
description: Treatment of buildings or structures in transition zones between exposure categories.

data.rule: For a building or structure located in the transition zone between exposure categories, the category resulting in the largest wind forces shall be used.
exceptions: An intermediate exposure between the preceding categories is permitted in a transition zone, provided it is determined by a rational analysis method defined in the recognized literature.

title: Exposure Requirements
description: Exposure requirements for different building code chapters and procedures.

data.rule: Exposure requirements for various wind load calculation procedures are specified in sections 26.7.4.1 through 26.7.4.4.

title: Directional Procedure (Chapter 27)
description: Exposure requirements for the Directional Procedure.

data.rule: For each wind direction considered, wind loads for the design of the MWFRS of enclosed and partially enclosed buildings using the Directional Procedure of Chapter 27 shall be based on the exposure as defined in Section 26.7.3. Wind loads for the design of open buildings with monoslope, pitched, or troughed free roofs shall be based on the exposure, as defined in Section 26.7.3, resulting in the highest wind loads for any wind direction at the site.

title: Envelope Procedure (Chapter 28)
description: Exposure requirements for the Envelope Procedure.

data.rule: Wind loads for the design of the MWFRS for all low-rise buildings designed using the Envelope Procedure of Chapter 28 shall be based on the exposure category resulting in the highest wind loads for any wind direction at the site.

title: Directional Procedure for Building Appurtenances and Other Structures (Chapter 29)
description: Exposure requirements for building appurtenances and other structures.

data.rule: Wind loads for the design of building appurtenances (such as rooftop structures and equipment) and other structures (such as solid freestanding walls and freestanding signs, chimneys, tanks, open signs, single-plane open frames, and trussed towers) as specified in Chapter 29 shall be based on the appropriate exposure for each wind direction considered.

title: Components and Cladding (Chapter 30)
description: Exposure requirements for components and cladding.

data.rule: Design wind pressures for C&C shall be based on the exposure category resulting in the highest wind loads for any wind direction at the site.

title: Topographic Effects
description: General provision for topographic effects on wind speed.

data.rule: Wind speed-up effects at isolated hills, ridges, and escarpments constituting abrupt changes in the general topography, located in any exposure category, shall be included in the determination of the wind loads when site conditions and locations of buildings and other structures meet all the following conditions: 1. The building or other structure is located as shown in Figure 26.8-1 in the upper one-half of a hill or ridge or near the crest of an escarpment. 2. H/Lh >= 0.2. 3. H is greater than or equal to 15 ft (4.5 m) for Exposures C and D and 60 ft (18 m) for Exposure B.

title: Topographic Factor
description: The wind speed-up effect shall be included in the calculation of design wind loads by using the factor Kzt.

data.rule: The wind speed-up effect shall be included in the calculation of design wind loads by using the factor Kzt. Kzt is determined from Eq. (26.8-1): Kzt = (1 + K1*K2*K3)^2, where K1, K2, and K3 are given in Figure 26.8-1. The values for K1 and K3 shall not be less than 0. The equations for K1, K2, and K3 may be used instead of using the tabulated values when increased accuracy in determining Kzt is required. For H/Lh > 0.5, assume that H/Lh = 0.5 for evaluating K1 and substitute 2H for Lh for evaluating K2 and K3. If site conditions and locations of buildings and other structures do not meet all the conditions specified in Section 26.8.1, then Kzt = 1.0.

title: Eq. 26.8-1: Topographic Factor Kzt
description: Equation for topographic factor Kzt used in wind speed-up calculations.

title: K2 equation for topographic multiplier
description: Equation for K2 factor to account for reduction in speed-up with distance upwind or downwind of crest.

x.source: Distance (upward or downward) from the crest to the site of the building or other structure

Lh.source: Distance upwind of crest to where the difference in ground elevation is half the height of hill or escarpment

title: K3 equation for topographic multiplier
description: Equation for K3 factor (height attenuation) to account for reduction in speed-up with height above local terrain.

z.source: Height above ground surface at the site of the building or other structure

Lh.source: Distance upwind of crest to where the difference in ground elevation is half the height of hill or escarpment

title: Figure 26.8-1: Topographic Factor Kzt - Topographic Multipliers Table
description: Topographic multipliers K1, K2, K3 for hills, ridges, and escarpments. Footnotes: (a) For values of H/Lh, x/Lh, and z/Lh other than those shown, linear interpolation is permitted. (b) For H/Lh > 0.5, assume that H/Lh = 0.5 for evaluating K1 and substitute 2H for Lh for evaluating K2 and K3. (c) Multipliers are based on the assumption that wind approaches the hill or escarpment along the direction of maximum slope. (d) Multipliers shall be used for any exposure.

figure_class.description: Table of topographic multipliers K1, K2, and K3 for computing Kzt for hills, ridges, and escarpments.

columns.name: K2: All Other Cases (Upwind and Downwind)

title: Figure 26.8-1: Parameters for Speed-Up over Hills and Escarpments
description: Parameters K1/(H/Lh), mu, and gamma for various hill shapes and exposure categories.

rows.Hill Shape: 2D ridge (or valley with negative H or K1/(H/Lh))

title: Ground Elevation Factor
description: Determination of the ground elevation factor Ke.

data.rule: The ground elevation factor to adjust for air density, Ke, shall be determined in accordance with Table 26.9-1. It is permitted to take Ke = 1 for all elevations.

title: Table 26.9-1. Ground Elevation Factor, Ke
description: Ground elevation factor Ke based on ground elevation above sea level. Footnotes: (a) Conservative approximation: Ke = 1.0 is permitted in all cases. (b) Factor Ke shall be determined from Table 26.9-1 in all cases. It is permitted to use the following formula for all elevations: Ke = e^(-0.0000362*zg), where zg = ground elevation above sea level, ft; or Ke = e^(-0.000119*zg), where zg = ground elevation above sea level, m. (c) Ke is permitted to be taken as 1.00 in all cases.

columns.name: Ground Elevation above Sea Level, ft
Ground Elevation above Sea Level, m

title: Velocity Pressure
description: Overview of velocity pressure provisions.

data.rule: Velocity pressure is determined in accordance with Section 26.10.1 (Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficient) and Section 26.10.2 (Velocity Pressure).

title: Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficient
description: Determination of the velocity pressure exposure coefficient Kz or Kh.

data.rule: Based on the exposure category determined in Section 26.7.3, a velocity pressure exposure coefficient, Kz or Kh, as applicable, shall be determined from Table 26.10-1. For a site located in a transition zone between exposure categories that is near to a change in ground surface roughness, intermediate values of Kz or Kh between those shown in Table 26.10-1 are permitted provided they are determined by a rational analysis method defined in the recognized literature.

title: Velocity Pressure
description: Velocity pressure qz evaluated at height z above ground.

data.rule: Velocity pressure, qz, evaluated at height z above ground shall be calculated by the following equation.

title: Velocity Pressure (Customary Units)
description: Velocity pressure evaluated at height z, in lb/ft^2 (Eq. 26.10-1)

data.expression: qz = 0.00256 * Kz * Kzt * Kd * Ke * V^2

title: Velocity Pressure (SI Units)
description: Velocity pressure evaluated at height z, in N/m^2 (Eq. 26.10-1.SI)

data.expression: qz = 0.613 * Kz * Kzt * Kd * Ke * V^2

title: Table 26.10-1: Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficients Kz and Kh
description: Velocity pressure exposure coefficients for height above ground level z or h, for Exposure B, C, and D

columns.name: Height above Ground Level z or h (ft)
Height above Ground Level z or h (m)

title: Gust-Effect Factor for Rigid Structures
description: The gust-effect factor for a rigid building or other structure is permitted to be taken as 0.85

data.rule: The gust-effect factor for a rigid building or other structure is permitted to be taken as 0.85.

title: Frequency Determination for Rigid vs Flexible
description: Determination of whether a building or other structure is rigid or flexible as defined in Section 26.2

data.rule: To determine whether a building or other structure is rigid or flexible, the fundamental natural frequency n1 shall be established using the structural properties and deformational characteristics of the resisting elements in a properly substantiated analysis. Low-rise buildings, as defined in Section 26.2, are permitted to be considered rigid.
data.then: Establish n1 via structural analysis or classify as rigid if low-rise
exceptions: Low-rise buildings as defined in Section 26.2 are permitted to be considered rigid.

title: Limitations for Approximate Natural Frequency
description: Conditions under which approximate building natural frequency na may be used instead of a rigorous analysis

data.rule: As an alternative to performing an analysis to determine n1, the approximate building natural frequency na shall be permitted to be calculated in accordance with Section 26.11.3 for structural steel, concrete, or masonry buildings meeting the following requirements.
data.then: na may be used per Section 26.11.3

title: Effective Length for Approximate Natural Frequency
description: Effective length Leff in the direction under consideration (Eq. 26.11-1)

data.expression: Leff = sum(hi * Li, i=1..n) / sum(hi, i=1..n)

hi.source: height above grade of level i

Li.source: building length at level i parallel to the wind direction

title: Approximate Natural Frequency for Steel Moment-Resisting Frame Buildings (Customary)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Steel Moment-Resisting Frame Buildings (SI)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Concrete Moment-Resisting Frame Buildings (Customary)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Concrete Moment-Resisting Frame Buildings (SI)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Steel and Concrete Buildings with Lateral-Force-Resisting Systems (Customary)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Steel and Concrete Buildings with Lateral-Force-Resisting Systems (SI)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Concrete or Masonry Shear Wall Buildings (Customary)
Approximate Natural Frequency for Concrete or Masonry Shear Wall Buildings (SI)
Shear Wall Coefficient Cw
description: Coefficient Cw for use in approximate natural frequency of shear wall buildings

Ai.source: Horizontal cross-sectional area of shear wall i

n.source: Number of shear walls in the building effective in resisting lateral forces in the direction under consideration

title: Gust-Effect Factor for Rigid Buildings (Eq. 26.11-6)
description: Calculated gust-effect factor for rigid buildings or other structures

Iz_bar.source: Intensity of turbulence at equivalent height z_bar

title: Intensity of Turbulence (Eq. 26.11-7)
description: Intensity of turbulence at equivalent height z_bar

title: Intensity of Turbulence SI (Eq. 26.11-7.SI)
description: Intensity of turbulence at equivalent height z_bar in SI units

title: Table 26.11-1: Terrain Exposure Constants
description: Terrain exposure constants for Exposure B, C, and D in both Customary and SI units

title: Background Response Factor Q (Eq. 26.11-8)
description: Background response factor Q for gust-effect factor calculation

B.source: Horizontal dimension of building normal to wind

Lz_bar.source: Integral length scale of turbulence at equivalent height

title: Integral Length Scale of Turbulence (Eq. 26.11-9)
description: Integral length scale of turbulence at equivalent height z_bar (Customary)

title: Integral Length Scale of Turbulence SI (Eq. 26.11-9.SI)
description: Integral length scale of turbulence at equivalent height z_bar (SI)

title: Gust-Effect Factor for Flexible Buildings (Eq. 26.11-10)
description: Gust-effect factor for flexible buildings or other structures

title: Resonant Peak Factor gR (Eq. 26.11-11)
description: Peak factor for resonant response

title: Resonant Response Factor R (Eq. 26.11-12)
description: Resonant response factor squared

data.expression: R = sqrt(1 / beta * Rn * Rh * (0.53 + 0.47 * Rb))

title: Power Spectral Density Rn (Eq. 26.11-13)
description: Power spectral density of turbulence at the equivalent height and natural frequency

title: Reduced Frequency N1 (Eq. 26.11-14)
description: Reduced frequency for spectral density calculation

Vz_bar.source: Mean hourly wind speed at equivalent height

title: Size Effect Factors Rh and Rb (Eq. 26.11-15a and 26.11-15b)
description: Size effect factors related to height, breadth, and depth of building

data.expression: Rl = (1 / eta) - (1 / (2 * eta^2)) * (1 - exp(-2 * eta)) for eta > 0; Rl = 1 for eta = 0

title: eta_h for Rh (Eq. 26.11-15a)
description: eta values for computing Rh

title: eta_B for Rb (Eq. 26.11-15b)
description: eta values for computing Rb

title: Mean Hourly Wind Speed at Equivalent Height (Eq. 26.11-16)
description: Mean hourly wind speed at equivalent height z_bar, in ft/s or m/s

title: Mean Hourly Wind Speed at Equivalent Height SI (Eq. 26.11-16.SI)
description: Mean hourly wind speed at equivalent height z_bar in SI

title: Rational Analysis for Gust Effect Factor
description: In lieu of the procedure defined in Sections 26.11.4 and 26.11.5, determination of the gust-effect factor by any rational analysis defined in the recognized literature is permitted.

data.rule: In lieu of the procedure defined in Sections 26.11.4 and 26.11.5, determination of the gust-effect factor by any rational analysis defined in the recognized literature is permitted.

title: Limitations on Combined Gust-Effect Factors and Pressure Coefficients
description: When combined gust-effect factors and pressure coefficients GCp, GCpf, and GCpi are given as figures and tables, the gust-effect factor shall not be determined separately.

data.rule: When combined gust-effect factors and pressure coefficients GCp, GCpf, and GCpi are given as figures and tables, the gust-effect factor shall not be determined separately.

title: Enclosure Classification General
description: For the purpose of determining internal pressure coefficients, buildings and other structures for which internal pressure coefficients GCpi apply shall be classified as enclosed, partially enclosed, partially open, or open, as defined in Section 26.2.

data.rule: Buildings and other structures shall be classified as enclosed, partially enclosed, partially open, or open, as defined in Section 26.2. If a building or other structure satisfies both the 'open' and 'partially enclosed' enclosure classification definitions, it shall be classified as a 'partially open' building or other structure.

title: Openings Determination
description: A determination shall be made of the amount of openings in the building envelope for use in determining the enclosure classification.

data.rule: A determination shall be made of the amount of openings in the building envelope for use in determining the enclosure classification. To make this determination, each building wall shall be assessed on the windward wall for consideration of the amount of openings present with respect to the remaining building envelope.

title: Protection of Glazed Openings
description: Glazed openings in Risk Category II, III, or IV buildings located in hurricane-prone regions shall be protected as specified in this section.

data.rule: Glazed openings in Risk Category II, III, or IV buildings located in hurricane-prone regions shall be protected as specified in this section.
data.then: Glazed openings shall be protected

title: Wind-Borne Debris Region Glazed Openings
description: Glazed openings shall be protected in accordance with Section 26.12.3.2 in wind-borne debris regions.

data.rule: Glazed openings shall be protected in accordance with Section 26.12.3.2 in the following locations: (1) Within 1 mi (1.6 km) of the mean high water line where an Exposure D condition exists upwind of the waterline and the basic wind speed is equal to or greater than 130 mi/h (58 m/s); or (2) In areas where the basic wind speed is equal to or greater than 140 mi/h (63 m/s).
data.then: Glazed openings shall be protected per Section 26.12.3.2
exceptions: Glazing located more than 60 ft (18.3 m) above the ground and more than 30 ft (9.2 m) above aggregate-surfaced roofs, including mats with gravel or stone ballast, located within 1,500 ft (458 m) or the building shall be permitted to be unprotected.

title: Requirements for Glazed Opening Protection
description: Requirements for impact-protective systems and testing for glazed openings

data.rule: Glazing in buildings requiring protection shall be impact resistant or be protected with an impact-protective system. Impact-protective systems and impact-resistant glazing shall be subjected to missile test and cyclic pressure differential tests in accordance with ASTM E1996 as applicable. Testing to demonstrate compliance with ASTM E1996 shall be in accordance with ASTM E1886. Impact-resistant glazing and impact-protective systems shall comply with the peaceful criteria of Table 5 or Table 6 of ASTM E1996 based on the missile required by Table 5 or Table 6 of ASTM E1996.
data.then: Glazing shall be impact resistant or protected per ASTM E1996/E1886
exceptions: When approved, other testing methods and/or performance criteria shall be permitted to be used to demonstrate compliance with all provisions of this section.

title: Table 26.13-1: Internal Pressure Coefficient (GCpi)
description: Main Wind Force Resisting System and Components and Cladding (All Heights): Internal Pressure Coefficient (GCpi) for Enclosed, Partially Enclosed, Partially Open, and Open Buildings (Walls and Roof)

rows.Criteria for Enclosure Classification: Ao is less than the smaller of 0.01Ag or 4 ft^2 (0.37 m^2), and Ao/Agi <= 0.2
Ao is less than the smaller of 0.01Ag or 4 ft^2 (0.37 m^2), and Ao/Agi <= 0.2
Ao > 1.1Aoi and Aoi = the lesser of 0.01Ag or 4 ft^2 (0.37 m^2), and Ao/Agi <= 0.2
Ao > 1.1Aoi and Aoi = the lesser of 0.01Ag or 4 ft^2 (0.37 m^2), and Ao/Agi <= 0.2
A building that does not comply with Enclosed, Partially Enclosed, or Open classifications
A building that does not comply with Enclosed, Partially Enclosed, or Open classifications
Each wall is at least 80% open

title: Reduction Factor for Large-Volume Buildings Ri (Eq. 26.13-1)
description: Reduction factor Ri for internal pressure coefficient in large-volume partially enclosed buildings

data.expression: Ri = 1.0 or Ri = 0.5 * (1 + 1 / sqrt(1 + (Vi / (6952 * Ao)))), whichever is less than 1.0

Ao.source: Total area of openings in the building envelope (walls and roof)

title: ANSI/DASMA 115
description: Standard Method for Testing Sectional Doors, Rolling Doors, and Flexible Doors: Determination of Structural Performance under Missile Impact and Cyclic Wind Pressure

title: ANSI/DASMA 108
description: Standard Method for Testing Sectional Doors and Rolling Doors: Determination of Structural Performance under Uniform Static Air Pressure Difference, ASTM International, 2005

title: ASTM E1886
description: Standard Test Method for Performance of Exterior Windows, Curtain Walls, Doors, and Impact Protective Systems Impacted by Missile(s) and Exposed to Cyclic Pressure Differentials, ASTM, 2019

title: ASTM E1996
description: Standard Specification for Performance of Exterior Windows, Curtain Walls, Doors and Impact Protective Systems Impacted by Windborne Debris in Hurricanes, ASTM International, 2020

title: CAN/CSA A123.21
description: Standard Test Method for the Dynamic Wind Uplift Resistance of Membrane-Roofing Systems, CSA Group, 2014

title: ASTM E330
description: Standard Test Method for Structural Performance of Exterior Windows, Doors, Skylights, and Curtain Walls by Uniform Static Air Pressure Difference, ASTM International, 2017

title: ASTM E1996 (Impact Protective Systems)
description: Pressure, Door and Access Systems Manufacturers Association International, 2017

title: Risk Category for Design Wind Loads
description: The basic wind speed V used in determination of design wind loads on roofing, rooftop equipment, and other building appurtenances shall consider the Risk Category equal to the greater of the following

data.rule: The basic wind speed V used in determination of design wind loads on roofing, rooftop equipment, and other building appurtenances shall consider the Risk Category equal to the greater of: (1) Risk category for the building on which the equipment or appurtenance is located, or (2) Risk category for any building or other structure to which the equipment or appurtenance provides a necessary service.

title: Velocity Pressure Exposure Coefficient Kz Interpolation
description: Notes for Table 26.10-1 regarding interpolation and exposure determination

data.rule: Velocity pressure exposure coefficient Kz may be determined from the following formulas: For 15 ft <= z <= zg: Kz = 2.01 * (z / zg)^(2/alpha). For z < 15 ft: Kz = 2.01 * (15 / zg)^(2/alpha). Linear interpolation for intermediate values of height z is acceptable. alpha and zg are tabulated in Table 26.11-1. Exposure categories are defined in Section 26.7.
data.then: Kz = 2.01 * (z / zg)^(2/alpha)
